title: Make Chaos Mesh install opt-in in kind Quick Start
description:
# GitHub Issue #284: Make Chaos Mesh installation in the kind Quick Start opt-in via a flag

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Move the `chaos-mesh` HelmRepository + HelmRelease out of the always-on `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` resource list (`deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml:36,47`) and into a kind-only opt-in path so that `make deploy-infra` no longer installs Chaos Mesh by default. The fault-injection platform is only needed for `make e2e-chaos` (`Makefile:335-338`) and the `e2e-chaos` GitHub Actions matrix (`.github/workflows/ci.yaml:678-761`) — for everyone else (Quick Start onboarding, contributors iterating on the operator, the regular `e2e-operator` run) it adds image pulls (controller-manager + chaos-daemon DaemonSet + cert-manager-issued webhook cert), a `dependsOn: cert-manager` gate inside Phase 3 of the HelmRelease wait (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:908`), and a `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` pre-step (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:513-588`) that on Ubuntu CI runners can shell out to `apt-get install linux-modules-extra-$(uname -r)` — none of which moves the developer any closer to a working Keystone API.

Concrete steps: (1) Drop `sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` and `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` from `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` (the two files stay on disk — they are reused by the new opt-in overlay). Drop the `chaos-mesh` `Namespace` from `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml:44-53` and move it into the opt-in overlay alongside the HelmRelease so the privileged-PodSecurity label only lands when Chaos Mesh is actually deployed. (2) Add a new opt-in kind overlay at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` that pulls in `../../flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml`, `../../flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`, the `chaos-mesh` Namespace, and the existing kind-tuning patch currently at `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142` (containerd runtime, dashboard disabled, reduced resource requests). (3) Remove the chaos-mesh patch block from `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142` — it has no target once chaos-mesh is no longer in the base resource list. (4) Add `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` (default `false`) to `hack/deploy-infra.sh`, mirroring the `WITH_FLUX_CLI` pattern at `hack/install-test-deps.sh:292`. When unset/`false`: skip `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules`, drop `chaos-mesh` from the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` list, and do not apply the new overlay. When `true`: run kernel-module loading, `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` after the base overlay, and add `chaos-mesh` back to the Phase 3 wait list. (5) Set `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` in the `e2e-chaos` workflow's deploy step (`.github/workflows/ci.yaml:738-739` `Setup E2E infrastructure` composite action — pass through as an env var) so the chaos test matrix continues to find a deployed Chaos Mesh. The default `e2e-operator` workflow stays on `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false` and gets a faster cluster bring-up. (6) Make the chaos-mesh assertion in `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:169-190` (Step 5) conditional — either gate the whole step on a Chainsaw `bindings`/`with` flag set from a `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` env var, or split it out into a separate suite under `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/` that the chaos workflow runs and the operator workflow skips. (7) Update `docs/quick-start.md` Step 3 architecture snapshot (`docs/quick-start.md:186-204`) and the *What happens* table (`docs/quick-start.md:150-161`): drop `chaos-mesh` from the default snapshot, add a `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block that documents `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra`. (8) Update `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md:339-366` to mark Chaos Mesh as kind-opt-in and point at the new overlay path. (9) Update the architecture document `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` and the existing review pattern at `.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` to reflect that Chaos Mesh is now kind-only — production overlays are explicitly *not* the place where customers get a chaos engineering platform; that is a separate operational decision left to the operator's user.

**Motivation**: The Quick Start's stated value proposition is *\"a fresh kind cluster with a working Keystone API in 5–10 minutes\"* (`docs/quick-start.md:166`), but the `chaos-mesh` HelmRelease pulls down the controller-manager image, the chaos-daemon DaemonSet image, and waits on cert-manager to issue webhook TLS — none of which contributes to a developer's first `openstack token issue`. Empirically the `chaos-mesh` line in Phase 3 of `wait_for_helmreleases` is one of the slower releases to flip `Ready=True` because it sits behind `dependsOn: cert-manager` (`deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml:17-19`) and its DaemonSet has to schedule on the kind node *and* successfully bind-mount the containerd socket. On top of that, `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` (`hack/deploy-infra.sh:513-588`) is run unconditionally on every Linux host even when no chaos suite will execute — on a clean Ubuntu CI runner this can trigger an `apt-get update` + `apt-get install linux-modules-extra-$(uname -r)` round-trip that the comment block at `:553-558` explicitly justifies because the modules are otherwise missing. For the Quick Start user that whole code path is dead weight. The asymmetry is well-established already by other recent kind-only addons: Headlamp (CC-0017), the Flux Web UI (CC-0086 / #257), the OpenBao UI flip (CC-0082 / #246), and Envoy Gateway + the `openstack-gw` Gateway (CC-0088 / #265) all live exclusively under `deploy/kind/base/` and stay out of the production `deploy/flux-system/` resource list. Chaos Mesh is the last platform-level addon that is still installed unconditionally, and it is the most expensive of the bunch in startup time and host-side prerequisites. Flipping it to opt-in is a one-flag switch in `deploy-infra.sh` plus a Kustomize move — same shape as `WITH_FLUX_CLI` for the optional Flux CLI install (`hack/install-test-deps.sh:289-294`).

**Affected Areas**:
- `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` (drop `sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` and `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` from the `resources:` list — the files themselves stay so the new overlay can reference them directly)
- `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml:44-53` (move the `chaos-mesh` Namespace + privileged PodSecurity label out of the unconditional namespace bundle)
- `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` (new — references the two flux-system manifests, declares the `chaos-mesh` Namespace, hosts the existing kind-tuning patch from `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142`)
- `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` (remove the chaos-mesh `patches:` block at lines 108-142; remaining base overlay is unchanged)
- `hack/deploy-infra.sh` (add `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=\"${WITH_CHAOS_MESH:-false}\"` near the existing config block at `:49-63`; gate `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` (called at `:800`); drop `chaos-mesh` from the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` argument list at `:907-908` when the flag is `false`; apply `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` after Step 3 when the flag is `true`)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` (export `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` in the chaos job's `Setup E2E infrastructure` step around `:738-739` — the operator job around `:474` deliberately stays on the default `false`)
- `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yml` (thread `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` from the workflow input into the deploy-infra environment if the action wraps the env)
- `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:169-190` (gate or split out the Step 5 chaos-mesh assertion so the suite passes on a default `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false` cluster)
- `docs/quick-start.md:150-161, 186-204` (drop `chaos-mesh` from the architecture snapshot; add a `::: tip` block that documents `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` and the chaos-suite use case)
- `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md:23-67, 339-366` (move the Chaos Mesh entry into a kind-only addon section, point the *File* line at the new `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/` overlay, drop the chaos-mesh row from the always-on cross-reference table)
- `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` (note the new `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` prerequisite for running the chaos suites locally)
- `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` (update the deployment-prerequisite section to reflect the opt-in flag)
- `Makefile:335-338` (optional — pre-set `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` for the `e2e-chaos` target so a developer running `make e2e-chaos` after a default Quick Start deploy gets a clear actionable error or auto-deploys Chaos Mesh; the simplest version is a recipe-level `@WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true hack/deploy-infra.sh` reminder)
- `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` (the existing chaos-mesh diagnostic dumps must no-op when the namespace is absent, not exit non-zero)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster (no `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` set) finishes without ever creating a `chaos-mesh` namespace, HelmRelease, or pod; `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns `NotFound`
- [ ] `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` reaches the same end state as today: `HelmRelease/chaos-mesh` `Ready=True`, `Deployment/chaos-controller-manager` `Available`, `DaemonSet/chaos-daemon` `numberReady >= 1` — assertions identical to `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:172-190`
- [ ] Default `make deploy-infra` shaves measurable wall-clock time off Phase 3 (target: ≥ 30s on a warm-cache laptop, ≥ 60s on a cold CI runner that skips the `linux-modules-extra` install path); a before/after timing line in the PR description is sufficient evidence
- [ ] `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` does not run when `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false` — verified by inspecting the `deploy-infra` log (`Loading kernel modules for chaos-mesh NetworkChaos:` line is absent)
- [ ] `make e2e-chaos` against a cluster brought up with `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` passes the same suites it passes today (PodChaos and, where the host kernel cooperates, NetworkChaos); a cluster brought up with the default flag fails fast with a clear error pointing at the flag rather than dumping cryptic CRD-not-found messages
- [ ] The `e2e-chaos` GitHub Actions job sets `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`; the `e2e-operator` job does not — the latter's wall-clock time on the Blacksmith runner drops by at least the chaos-mesh HelmRelease wait
- [ ] `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` Step 5 either (a) runs only on `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` clusters via a Chainsaw binding, or (b) is moved to a sibling suite that the chaos workflow runs and the operator workflow does not — a default Quick Start cluster passes the remaining infra-stack-health assertions
- [ ] `docs/quick-start.md` no longer claims `chaos-mesh` is part of the default cluster snapshot at `:186-204`; a tip block documents the opt-in flag and links to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`
- [ ] `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` reclassifies Chaos Mesh as a kind-only addon and lists its overlay path (`deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/`), matching the documentation posture for Headlamp / Flux Web UI / Envoy Gateway
- [ ] `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` no longer references `sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` or `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` — production overlay never deploys Chaos Mesh implicitly
- [ ] `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml` no longer creates the `chaos-mesh` Namespace; the namespace is created by `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/` only when the flag is set

**Non-Goals**:
- Removing Chaos Mesh from the project. The CRDs, the chaos-suite tests under `tests/e2e-chaos/`, and the kind-tuning patch are all kept exactly as they are — the change is *when* Chaos Mesh is installed, not *whether* the project supports it.
- Reworking the `tests/e2e-chaos/` suites or the `chainsaw-config.yaml` for the chaos workflow. They keep working byte-for-byte once the deploy flag is set.
- Switching the chaos workflow's runner topology, caching, or NetworkChaos kernel-module handling. Those concerns are orthogonal to making chaos opt-in for everyone else.
- Building a new mode that auto-detects whether the chaos suite is about to run and toggles the flag. Explicit opt-in mirrors the existing `WITH_FLUX_CLI` pattern (`hack/install-test-deps.sh:292`) and avoids a magic-action coupling between the deploy script and the test runner.
- Adding Chaos Mesh to a prod overlay or any non-kind context. Production users who want a chaos engineering platform install it themselves on the same posture as Envoy Gateway / Headlamp / the OpenBao UI flip — those are deliberately kind-only conveniences.
- Making the `chaos-mesh` patch block in `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` survive in place \"just in case.\" Once the resource is gone from the base resource list the patch has no target and Kustomize will refuse to render — the patch must move into the new overlay where its target lives.

**References**:
- Existing kind-only addon prior art: CC-0082 / #246 (OpenBao UI), CC-0086 / #257 (Flux Web UI), CC-0088 / #265 (Envoy Gateway + `openstack-gw`)
- `WITH_FLUX_CLI` opt-in pattern: `hack/install-test-deps.sh:289-294` and `docs/quick-start.md:51-55`
- Chaos Mesh feature history: CC-0046 (initial Chaos Mesh deployment), CC-0049 (NetworkChaos kernel-module handling), CC-0054 (chaos E2E workflow split-out)
- Current chaos-mesh manifests: `deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml`, `deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`, `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142`
- Current kernel-module pre-step: `hack/deploy-infra.sh:513-588`, called unconditionally at `:800`
- Chaos suite consumer: `Makefile:335-338`, `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:678-761`, `tests/e2e-chaos/`
- Architecture context: `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md`, `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/284

stories.title: Quick Start user gets a fast cluster bring-up without Chaos Mesh
stories.role: developer following the kind Quick Start
stories.want: make deploy-infra to skip Chaos Mesh installation by default
stories.so_that: the cluster reaches a working Keystone API without paying the cost of chaos-mesh image pulls, the cert-manager-gated HelmRelease wait, and the load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules pre-step
criteria: After `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster, `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns NotFound
After `make deploy-infra`, `kubectl get helmrelease -A | grep chaos-mesh` is empty
The deploy-infra log does NOT contain the string `Loading kernel modules for chaos-mesh NetworkChaos:`
Phase 3 of the deploy-infra log no longer lists `chaos-mesh` in `wait_for_helmreleases`
End-to-end wall-clock time for `make deploy-infra` is shorter than today by at least the chaos-mesh HelmRelease wait, recorded as a before/after timing line in the PR description (CC-0097)

stories.title: Chaos engineer opts in to Chaos Mesh via WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true
stories.role: developer running the chaos E2E suites locally
stories.want: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra to install Chaos Mesh end-to-end
stories.so_that: the e2e-chaos suites under tests/e2e-chaos/ run unchanged after I bring up the cluster
criteria: `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` reaches end state with `HelmRelease/chaos-mesh` Ready=True, `Deployment/chaos-controller-manager` Available, `DaemonSet/chaos-daemon` numberReady >= 1 (CC-0097)
`load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` runs only when WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true — verified by the `Loading kernel modules` log line being present
`kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` is invoked exactly once after the base overlay is applied, when the flag is true
`chaos-mesh` is added back to the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` argument list when the flag is true
`make e2e-chaos` against this cluster passes the same suites it passes today (PodChaos always; NetworkChaos where the host kernel cooperates)

stories.title: CI's e2e-chaos workflow continues to find a deployed Chaos Mesh; e2e-operator stops paying for it
stories.want: the e2e-chaos GitHub Actions matrix to set WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true while the regular e2e-operator/e2e-infra jobs leave it default-false
stories.so_that: chaos suites have the platform they need and the default operator pipeline shaves the chaos-mesh HelmRelease wait off every CI run
criteria: The `e2e-chaos` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` exports `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` to the `Setup E2E infrastructure` step
The `e2e-infra` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` does NOT set WITH_CHAOS_MESH (relies on the default false)
The `Setup E2E infrastructure` composite action threads `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` from its calling job's env into the `Deploy infrastructure stack` step env
Both matrix entries (`pod` and `network`) of e2e-chaos receive `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`
After the change, the e2e-chaos matrix still runs all test_dirs listed in ci.yaml without `CRD-not-found` errors

stories.title: Chainsaw infra-stack-health suite passes on a default Quick Start cluster
stories.role: developer running tests/e2e/infrastructure/ against a default kind cluster
stories.want: the existing chaos-mesh assertions in infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml Step 5 to NOT fail when Chaos Mesh is not installed
stories.so_that: the default infra suite passes after a vanilla `make deploy-infra` and the chaos-mesh assertions still run when WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true
criteria: The chaos-mesh assertions live in a sibling suite at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` (or are gated in place via a presence-check script and `overlay: kind` label) so they are skipped on default clusters
The new/modified suite uses the same `overlay: kind` label gating + runtime presence-check pattern as `tests/e2e/infrastructure/flux-web-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` (CC-0086)
The assertions still cover: `Deployment/chaos-controller-manager` availableReplicas > 0 AND `DaemonSet/chaos-daemon` numberReady >= 1 (matching today's lines :172-190)
Running `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/` against a WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false cluster passes
Running the same command against a WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true cluster also passes (chaos-mesh-health assertions exercise the live deployment)

stories.title: Documentation reflects Chaos Mesh as a kind-only opt-in addon
stories.want: docs/quick-start.md and docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md to NOT claim that Chaos Mesh is part of the default cluster
stories.so_that: I am not surprised by chaos-mesh pods after deploy-infra, and I know the WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true flag if I want chaos suites locally
criteria: `docs/quick-start.md` `What happens` table (lines 150-161) and `What gets deployed` snapshot (lines 186-204) no longer include `chaos-mesh` in the default flow
A `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block in `docs/quick-start.md` documents `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` and links to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`

`docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` reclassifies Chaos Mesh as a kind-only addon, with the *File* line pointing at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/` overlay (matching the documentation posture for Headlamp / Flux Web UI / Envoy Gateway)
`docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` HelmRelease–HelmRepository cross-reference table no longer lists chaos-mesh under the always-on flux-system production overlay
`docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` documents `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` as a prerequisite for running the chaos suites locally
`architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` deployment-prerequisite section reflects the opt-in flag

`.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` is updated so the Chaos Mesh example reflects that the overlay is now kind-only (production explicitly does NOT install chaos-mesh)

stories.title: Operator running `make e2e-chaos` against a default cluster gets a clear, actionable error
stories.role: developer who forgot to set the flag
stories.want: `make e2e-chaos` to fail fast with a message pointing at WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true rather than dumping cryptic CRD-not-found stack traces
stories.so_that: I can fix the missing flag in one step instead of digging through chainsaw output
criteria: The `e2e-chaos` Makefile target detects an absent chaos-mesh installation and exits non-zero with a single-line message: `chaos-mesh is not installed; run \`WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra\` first` (or equivalent wording)
The check is a fast `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` (or equivalent CRD presence check) — not a chainsaw run
The check does NOT auto-deploy chaos-mesh (that is explicitly out of scope per Non-Goals)
When chaos-mesh IS installed, the target proceeds to chainsaw exactly as today

stories.title: Diagnostics dump script tolerates an absent chaos-mesh namespace
stories.role: CI/dev investigating a failed run on a default cluster
stories.want: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` to no-op the `chaos-daemon` describe when the namespace is absent rather than exit non-zero
stories.so_that: diagnostic dumps from the default e2e-infra job continue to work without spurious failures
criteria: Running `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` against a WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false cluster exits 0
The `chaos-daemon` describe block guards on `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` (or equivalent) and prints a single skip line when the namespace is missing
When chaos-mesh IS deployed, the existing describe output is unchanged

requirements.description: The `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` SHALL no longer reference `sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` or `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` so production overlays never deploy Chaos Mesh implicitly. The two files themselves SHALL stay on disk and be referenced from the new opt-in overlay.
requirements.rationale: The base flux-system overlay is the production posture; Chaos Mesh is a developer-only fault-injection platform and its presence there causes implicit deployment in every prod-shaped overlay (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: Production overlay no longer renders chaos-mesh resources
scenarios.then: no `HelmRepository`, `HelmRelease`, or `Namespace` named `chaos-mesh` is in the output
and_then: the `Namespace/chaos-mesh` block is gone from `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml`
the build still succeeds (no orphan references)

scenarios.name: On-disk chaos-mesh source/release files survive
scenarios.then: both files exist with their existing content
and_then: the new overlay at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` references both via relative paths (`../../flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` and `../../flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`)

scenarios.name: Default kind overlay still does not pull chaos-mesh
scenarios.then: no chaos-mesh resources appear in the output
and_then: no kustomize patch targets a missing chaos-mesh HelmRelease (the base overlay's chaos-mesh patch block is removed)

requirements.description: A new opt-in kind overlay SHALL exist at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` that bundles the `chaos-mesh` Namespace (with privileged PodSecurity label), the existing HelmRepository (`sources/chaos-mesh.yaml`), the existing HelmRelease (`releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`), and the kind-tuning patch currently at `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142`.
requirements.rationale: Kustomize requires the patch target to exist; relocating both the resources and the patch into one overlay keeps the bundle cohesive and matches the prior-art posture used for Headlamp / Flux Web UI / Envoy Gateway (CC-0017, CC-0086, CC-0088)

scenarios.name: Overlay renders all chaos-mesh resources
scenarios.then: the output contains `Namespace/chaos-mesh` (with the privileged PodSecurity label), `HelmRepository/chaos-mesh`, and `HelmRelease/chaos-mesh` with the kind-tuning values applied (containerd runtime, dashboard disabled, reduced resource requests)
and_then: the build exits 0
the rendered HelmRelease's `dependsOn: cert-manager` is preserved verbatim from `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`

scenarios.name: Patch target exists
scenarios.when: the kind-tuning patch in the new overlay is rendered
scenarios.then: kustomize finds its target HelmRelease in the same overlay (no `target not found` error)

scenarios.name: Privileged PodSecurity label preserved
scenarios.when: the rendered Namespace/chaos-mesh manifest is inspected
scenarios.then: `metadata.labels."pod-security.kubernetes.io/enforce"` is `privileged` (matching today's `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml:44-53`)
and_then: a comment in the namespace block preserves the rationale (chaos-daemon needs host PID/network and containerd socket)

requirements.description: `hack/deploy-infra.sh` SHALL define `WITH_CHAOS_MESH="${WITH_CHAOS_MESH:-false}"` near the existing config block at lines 49-63 and gate three behaviors on it: the `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` call, the inclusion of `chaos-mesh` in the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` argument list, and a new `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` step that runs after the base overlay is applied.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the WITH_FLUX_CLI opt-in pattern at hack/install-test-deps.sh:289-294. A single environment variable gives the user one knob to flip; defaulting to false keeps the Quick Start fast (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: Default false skips all three chaos-mesh code paths
scenarios.then: `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` is NOT called, `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` is NOT executed, and Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` is invoked WITHOUT `chaos-mesh` in its argument list
and_then: the script's printed config block reports `WITH_CHAOS_MESH: false`
the deploy-infra log is free of any `chaos-mesh` mention apart from the config echo

scenarios.name: Explicit true opts in to all three code paths
scenarios.then: `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` runs, `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` runs after the base overlay, and Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` includes `chaos-mesh` in its argument list
and_then: the script's printed config block reports `WITH_CHAOS_MESH: true`

scenarios.name: Explicit false matches default false
scenarios.then: behavior is identical to the unset case

scenarios.name: Invalid value is treated as false (defensive)
scenarios.when: the script runs with `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=yes` or any non-`true` value
scenarios.then: the gates behave as if false (no install)
and_then: this matches the WITH_FLUX_CLI pattern that compares with the literal string `true`

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` SHALL set `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` for both matrix entries (`pod` and `network`) before the `Setup E2E infrastructure` step runs; the `e2e-infra` job SHALL NOT set this flag.
requirements.rationale: Chaos suites need a deployed Chaos Mesh; the operator/infra job no longer needs it and benefits from a faster bring-up (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: e2e-chaos exports the flag
scenarios.then: `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` is in the env passed to the composite action
and_then: both matrix entries (suite=pod and suite=network) receive the flag identically

scenarios.name: e2e-infra does not export the flag
scenarios.then: WITH_CHAOS_MESH is NOT set, falling back to the script default of false
and_then: the deploy-infra wall-clock time on the Blacksmith runner drops by at least the chaos-mesh HelmRelease wait

scenarios.name: Composite action threads the flag
scenarios.when: the `Setup E2E infrastructure` composite action runs the `Deploy infrastructure stack` step
scenarios.then: the env block of that step includes `WITH_CHAOS_MESH: ${{ env.WITH_CHAOS_MESH }}` (or equivalent passthrough)
and_then: an unset WITH_CHAOS_MESH in the calling job translates to an empty/false value in the deploy-infra env

requirements.description: The chaos-mesh assertion currently at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:169-190` (Step 5) SHALL no longer fail on a default `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false` cluster. The chaos-mesh assertions SHALL be moved into a sibling suite at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` carrying `metadata.labels.overlay: kind` and a runtime presence-check, modeled on the `flux-web-health` pattern (CC-0086).
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the precedent set by CC-0086 for the Flux Web UI: a kind-only assertion lives in its own labelled sibling test that is default-safe via a runtime presence guard

scenarios.name: Default cluster passes infra-stack-health
scenarios.when: `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e/infrastructure/` runs against a WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false cluster
scenarios.then: all suites pass — the new chaos-mesh-health suite short-circuits via its runtime presence guard with `SKIP: chaos-mesh ... not present`
and_then: infra-stack-health no longer carries the chaos-mesh Step 5 lines :169-190
the remaining infra-stack-health steps (1-4, 6) run unchanged

scenarios.name: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true cluster exercises the new suite
scenarios.when: the same chainsaw command runs against a cluster where `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` was invoked
scenarios.then: the chaos-mesh-health suite asserts `Deployment/chaos-controller-manager.status.availableReplicas > 0` AND `DaemonSet/chaos-daemon.status.numberReady >= 1`
and_then: both assertions hit a real chaos-mesh deployment and pass

scenarios.name: Selector-based exclusion still works
scenarios.then: chaos-mesh-health is excluded by label selector before any presence check runs
and_then: this matches the dual-gate pattern of flux-web-health: label gate for callers, presence guard for forgotten selectors

requirements.description: Documentation SHALL reflect Chaos Mesh as a kind-only opt-in addon. `docs/quick-start.md` SHALL drop chaos-mesh from the default-flow descriptions, add a `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block that documents the `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` invocation, and link to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`. `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` SHALL reclassify Chaos Mesh as a kind-only addon and point at the new overlay path. `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` and `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` SHALL note the new opt-in prerequisite.
requirements.rationale: The docs currently promise that chaos-mesh is part of the default cluster. Leaving them stale would cause Quick Start readers to assume something is broken when they don't see chaos-mesh after deploy-infra (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: Quick Start no longer claims chaos-mesh by default
scenarios.when: a reader scans `docs/quick-start.md` Step 3 and the *What gets deployed* details block
scenarios.then: no `chaos-mesh` namespace appears in the architecture snapshot, and a `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block calls out `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra`
and_then: the tip block links to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`
the *What happens* table has no row that introduces chaos-mesh

scenarios.name: infrastructure-manifests reclassifies chaos-mesh
scenarios.then: the Chaos Mesh entry's *File* line points at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`, and the entry sits under a kind-only addon section (matching the prior art for Headlamp / Flux Web UI / Envoy Gateway)
and_then: the chaos-mesh row is removed from the always-on HelmRelease–HelmRepository cross-reference table
the `Namespaces` table no longer lists `chaos-mesh`

scenarios.name: chaos-e2e-tests notes the prerequisite
scenarios.when: a reader reaches the Quick Start / Setup section of `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md`
scenarios.then: a single sentence states `Run \`WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra\` before \`make e2e-chaos\``
and_then: the architecture document `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` Infrastructure Setup section is updated to reflect the opt-in flag (replaces the `SKIP_CHAOS_MESH=true` reference with `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`)

requirements.description: The `e2e-chaos` Makefile target SHALL fail fast with a single, actionable error message when chaos-mesh is not installed, before invoking chainsaw, and SHALL NOT auto-deploy chaos-mesh.
requirements.rationale: A developer running `make e2e-chaos` after a default Quick Start would otherwise see a cryptic chainsaw failure on missing CRDs. A one-line preflight message points them at the flag instead (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: Missing chaos-mesh fails fast with actionable message
scenarios.when: `make e2e-chaos` runs against a cluster where `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns NotFound
scenarios.then: the target exits non-zero with a single-line message naming `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra`
and_then: the chainsaw process is never invoked

scenarios.name: Installed chaos-mesh runs chainsaw normally
scenarios.when: `make e2e-chaos` runs against a cluster where the chaos-mesh namespace exists
scenarios.then: the target proceeds to `chainsaw test --config tests/e2e-chaos/chainsaw-config.yaml tests/e2e-chaos/` exactly as today

scenarios.name: No auto-deploy attempted
scenarios.then: the target does NOT shell out to `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true hack/deploy-infra.sh` (explicit opt-in only)
and_then: this preserves the explicit-opt-in design and avoids hidden cluster mutations

requirements.description: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` SHALL no-op the `chaos-daemon` describe (and any other chaos-mesh diagnostic block) when the `chaos-mesh` namespace is absent, exiting 0.
requirements.rationale: Diagnostic dumps must work on default Quick Start clusters where chaos-mesh is no longer installed (CC-0097)

scenarios.name: Default cluster diagnostics succeed
scenarios.when: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` runs against a cluster where `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns NotFound
scenarios.then: the script exits 0 and the chaos-daemon describe block prints a single skip line
and_then: the rest of the diagnostic dump (HelmReleases, Pods, Events, FluxInstance/FluxReport, operator section) runs unchanged

scenarios.name: Chaos-installed cluster diagnostics emit chaos-daemon detail
scenarios.when: the script runs against a cluster where chaos-mesh is deployed
scenarios.then: the chaos-daemon describe block emits today's output (`kubectl describe daemonset -n chaos-mesh chaos-daemon`)

scenarios.name: Set -e safety
scenarios.when: the chaos-daemon describe presence guard runs under `set -euo pipefail`
scenarios.then: a missing namespace does NOT abort the script (the guard returns 0 by design)

requirements.description: The chaos-mesh kind-tuning patch (containerd runtime, disabled dashboard, reduced resource requests) SHALL be removed from `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` and SHALL exist only in the new `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` overlay.
requirements.rationale: Once chaos-mesh is no longer in the base resource list, the patch has no target and kustomize will refuse to render the base overlay (CC-0097 Non-Goal: 'survive in place just in case')

scenarios.name: Base overlay no longer carries the chaos-mesh patch
scenarios.then: no patch targets `HelmRelease/chaos-mesh` in the rendered output and the build succeeds
and_then: lines 108-142 of `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml` are gone

scenarios.name: Kind chaos-mesh overlay carries the patch verbatim
scenarios.then: the rendered HelmRelease has `chaosDaemon.runtime: containerd`, `chaosDaemon.socketPath: /run/containerd/containerd.sock`, `dashboard.create: false`, and the reduced resource requests/limits previously at base lines 108-142
and_then: the patch target name/namespace match the HelmRelease in the same overlay

tasks.title: Drop chaos-mesh entries from `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml` (`sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` line 36, `releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` line 47); leave the two referenced files untouched on disk for reuse by the new overlay (REQ-001, CC-0097)

Remove the `chaos-mesh` Namespace block (lines 44-53) from `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml` so the privileged-PodSecurity label only lands when the kind chaos-mesh overlay is applied (REQ-001, CC-0097)

Create `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` referencing `../../flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml`, `../../flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml`, and an inline `Namespace/chaos-mesh` (with privileged PodSecurity label + rationale comment); host the kind-tuning HelmRelease patch (containerd runtime, dashboard disabled, reduced resource requests) verbatim from current base lines 108-142 (REQ-002, REQ-009, CC-0097)
Remove the chaos-mesh patch block at `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142` so kustomize no longer references a missing target (REQ-009, CC-0097)

Add `tests/unit/deploy/test_kustomize_render.bats` (or extend an existing kustomize-render test if present) covering: (a) `kustomize build deploy/flux-system` emits no chaos-mesh resources, (b) `kustomize build deploy/kind/base` succeeds with no chaos-mesh resources and no orphan patch error, (c) `kustomize build deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` emits Namespace + HelmRepository + HelmRelease with kind-tuning values applied and `dependsOn: cert-manager` preserved (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-009, CC-0097)

Add `WITH_CHAOS_MESH="${WITH_CHAOS_MESH:-false}"` to the config block of `hack/deploy-infra.sh` near lines 49-63; emit it in the `Deploy Infrastructure to Kind Cluster` config echo at line 788+ so the value is visible in CI logs (REQ-003, CC-0097)
Gate the `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules` call (currently unconditional at `hack/deploy-infra.sh:800`) on `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`; preserve the existing function body unchanged (REQ-003, CC-0097)

After `kubectl apply -k "${REPO_ROOT}/deploy/kind/base"` in Step 3 (`hack/deploy-infra.sh` near line 877), conditionally run `kubectl apply -k "${REPO_ROOT}/deploy/kind/chaos-mesh"` when `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`; log the action (REQ-003, CC-0097)

Refactor the Phase 3 `wait_for_helmreleases` invocation at `hack/deploy-infra.sh:907-908` to build the argument list dynamically — append `chaos-mesh` only when `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true`; preserve order of remaining releases (REQ-003, CC-0097)

Add `tests/unit/hack/test_deploy_infra_chaos_flag.bats` exercising default-false skip, explicit-true install, explicit-false skip, and non-`true` (e.g. `yes`) defensive-skip cases by stubbing `load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules`, `kubectl`, and `wait_for_helmreleases` and asserting which invocations occurred (REQ-003, CC-0097)

Update `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` chaos-daemon block (line 38-39) to guard with `if kubectl get ns chaos-mesh >/dev/null 2>&1; then ...; else echo 'SKIP: chaos-mesh namespace not present'; fi` (REQ-008, CC-0097)

Add `tests/unit/hack/test_ci_dump_diagnostics_chaos_absent.bats` covering both absent-namespace (skip line, exit 0) and present-namespace (describe invocation) cases via stubbed kubectl (REQ-008, CC-0097)

Update `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` to thread `WITH_CHAOS_MESH` from the calling job's env into the `Deploy infrastructure stack` step's env (alongside the existing `SKIP_KIND_CREATE`); add a header comment referencing CC-0097 (REQ-004)

Update `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` `e2e-chaos` job (around line 738-739) to set `env: WITH_CHAOS_MESH: "true"` on the `Setup E2E infrastructure` step for both matrix entries; verify the `e2e-infra` job (around line 439-440) does NOT set the variable so it falls back to false (REQ-004, CC-0097)

Add `tests/integration/ci/test_setup_e2e_infra_threading.bats` (or extend an existing CI-action contract test) covering: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true threads through to deploy-infra env; unset value does not leak into deploy-infra env (REQ-004, CC-0097)

Create `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` modeled on `tests/e2e/infrastructure/flux-web-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` (CC-0086): `metadata.labels.overlay: kind`, single `script:` step bash with `set -euo pipefail`, presence guard `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh`, then `kubectl wait --for=condition=Available deployment/chaos-controller-manager -n chaos-mesh` and `kubectl rollout status daemonset/chaos-daemon -n chaos-mesh` (or asserts equivalent to today's lines 172-190); `catch:` describes both resources (REQ-005, CC-0097)

Remove Step 5 chaos-mesh assertions (lines 169-190) from `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml`; renumber surrounding step comments and update the file header to mention the sibling chaos-mesh-health suite — mirror the flux-web-health header pattern (REQ-005, CC-0097)

Update the `.PHONY: e2e-chaos` recipe in `Makefile` (around lines 335-338) to add a single-line preflight: `@kubectl get ns chaos-mesh >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'chaos-mesh is not installed; run \`WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra\` first'; exit 1; }` before the chainsaw invocation (REQ-007, CC-0097)

Add `tests/unit/Makefile/test_e2e_chaos_preflight.bats` exercising the missing-namespace fast-fail (exit non-zero, message on stderr, no chainsaw) and present-namespace happy path via stubbed kubectl + chainsaw (REQ-007, CC-0097)

Update `docs/quick-start.md` Step 3 (`What happens` table at lines 150-161 and `What gets deployed` snapshot at lines 186-204) to drop chaos-mesh from the default flow; add a new `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block documenting `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` and linking to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` (REQ-006, CC-0097)

Update `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md`: move the Chaos Mesh entry to a `### Kind-only addons` subsection alongside Headlamp/Flux Web UI/Envoy Gateway prior art; change *File* line to `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`; remove the chaos-mesh row from the always-on HelmRelease–HelmRepository cross-reference table; remove `chaos-mesh` from the Namespaces table at lines 58-67; add a note explaining the WITH_CHAOS_MESH flag (REQ-006, CC-0097)

Update `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` Setup/Quick Start section to add a single-sentence prerequisite: `Run \`WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra\` before \`make e2e-chaos\``; cross-link to `docs/quick-start.md` tip block (REQ-006, CC-0097)

Update `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` Infrastructure Setup section: replace the `SKIP_CHAOS_MESH=true` reference with the new `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true` opt-in flag; explain that production overlays explicitly do NOT install chaos-mesh (REQ-006, CC-0097)

Update `.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` to reflect that the Chaos Mesh kind-tuning patch now lives in a kind-only overlay (production explicitly omits chaos-mesh entirely); preserve the original CC-0046 example block as historical record but add a CC-0097 follow-up note (REQ-006, CC-0097)

Add `tests/unit/docs/test_quick_start_no_chaos_default.py` and `tests/unit/docs/test_infrastructure_manifests_chaos_kind_only.py` (or extend equivalent doc-conformance tests if present) that parse the markdown and assert the contracts in REQ-006 (CC-0097)

End-to-end manual verification (recorded in PR description per acceptance criteria): time `make deploy-infra` and `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` on a clean kind cluster; capture before/after timing line; confirm `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns NotFound on the default run and Ready resources on the opt-in run; confirm `make e2e-chaos` against the opt-in cluster passes the same suites it passes today (CC-0097)

test_specifications.test_function: chaos-mesh-health/Step1: HelmRelease + Deployment + DaemonSet on chaos-installed cluster [e2e]
test_specifications.story: Chaos engineer opts in to Chaos Mesh via WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true
test_specifications.expected: On a cluster with WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true: HelmRelease/chaos-mesh Ready=True, Deployment/chaos-controller-manager availableReplicas > 0, DaemonSet/chaos-daemon numberReady >= 1

test_specifications.test_function: chaos-mesh-health/Step0: presence-check skip on default cluster [e2e]
test_specifications.story: Chainsaw infra-stack-health suite passes on a default Quick Start cluster
test_specifications.expected: If `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` returns NotFound, the script emits `SKIP: chaos-mesh namespace not present.` and exits 0

test_specifications.test_function: infra-stack-health: Step 5 (chaos-mesh) is removed from the always-on suite [e2e]
test_specifications.story: Chainsaw infra-stack-health suite passes on a default Quick Start cluster
test_specifications.expected: The chainsaw-test.yaml no longer includes the Step 5 chaos-mesh assertions; the file passes against a default cluster

test_specifications.test_function: deploy-infra: WITH_CHAOS_MESH default false skips kernel modules, overlay apply, and Phase 3 chaos-mesh wait [unit]
test_specifications.story: Quick Start user gets a fast cluster bring-up without Chaos Mesh

test_specifications.test_function: deploy-infra: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true triggers all three code paths [unit]

test_specifications.expected: With WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true, mocks observe load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules invocation, a `kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` call after the base overlay, and `chaos-mesh` present in the Phase 3 wait_for_helmreleases argument list

test_specifications.test_function: deploy-infra: WITH_CHAOS_MESH=yes treated as false (defensive parity with WITH_FLUX_CLI) [unit]

test_specifications.expected: With any value other than the literal string `true`, the chaos-mesh code paths are skipped (matches the equality test pattern at hack/install-test-deps.sh:292)

test_specifications.expected: `kustomize build deploy/flux-system | grep -E 'chaos-mesh'` returns no resource (no Namespace, no HelmRepository, no HelmRelease)

test_specifications.test_function: kustomize build deploy/kind/base: no chaos-mesh resources, no orphan patch [unit]

test_specifications.expected: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base` succeeds (exit 0); rendered output contains zero chaos-mesh resources; no `target not found` error from the (now-removed) patch block

test_specifications.story: CI's e2e-chaos workflow continues to find a deployed Chaos Mesh; e2e-operator stops paying for it

test_specifications.expected: With a stub kubectl returning the chaos-mesh namespace, the script invokes `kubectl describe daemonset -n chaos-mesh chaos-daemon` exactly once

test_specifications.story: Documentation reflects Chaos Mesh as a kind-only opt-in addon
test_specifications.expected: Parsing `docs/quick-start.md`: the *What gets deployed* code fence contains no `chaos-mesh` substring; a `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block exists and mentions `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra`

test_specifications.test_function: docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md: Chaos Mesh entry points at deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/ [unit]
test_specifications.story: Documentation reflects Chaos Mesh as a kind-only opt-in addon
test_specifications.expected: Parsing the file: the Chaos Mesh entry's *File:* line is `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`; the always-on HelmRelease–HelmRepository cross-reference table no longer contains a `chaos-mesh` row; the Namespaces table no longer lists `chaos-mesh`

review_criteria: BLOCKER: `kustomize build deploy/flux-system` produces zero chaos-mesh resources (no Namespace, HelmRepository, or HelmRelease) — REQ-001
BLOCKER: `kustomize build deploy/kind/base` succeeds with no chaos-mesh resources and no orphan patch error — REQ-009

BLOCKER: `kustomize build deploy/kind/chaos-mesh` succeeds and emits Namespace (privileged label preserved) + HelmRepository + HelmRelease with kind-tuning values applied and `dependsOn: cert-manager` intact — REQ-002, REQ-009

BLOCKER: `make deploy-infra` on a fresh kind cluster (no env override) finishes without creating a chaos-mesh namespace; the deploy-infra log is free of `Loading kernel modules for chaos-mesh NetworkChaos:` — REQ-003

BLOCKER: `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` reaches end state with HelmRelease/chaos-mesh Ready=True, Deployment/chaos-controller-manager Available, DaemonSet/chaos-daemon numberReady >= 1 — REQ-003
BLOCKER: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` no longer asserts chaos-mesh resources and passes against a default cluster — REQ-005

BLOCKER: `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` exists, carries `metadata.labels.overlay: kind`, runs the same assertions as the removed Step 5 against a chaos-installed cluster, and short-circuits via runtime presence guard on a default cluster — REQ-005
CRITICAL: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` e2e-chaos job sets WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true for both matrix entries; e2e-infra job does NOT set it — REQ-004
CRITICAL: `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` threads WITH_CHAOS_MESH from calling job env into the deploy-infra step env — REQ-004
CRITICAL: `make e2e-chaos` against a default cluster fails fast with a single-line message naming `WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra` and never invokes chainsaw — REQ-007

CRITICAL: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` exits 0 against a default cluster and emits a single SKIP line for the chaos-daemon block; emits the existing describe output against a chaos-installed cluster — REQ-008

MINOR: `docs/quick-start.md` no longer claims chaos-mesh in the default cluster snapshot at lines 186-204; a `::: tip Enabling Chaos Mesh` block documents the flag and links to `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md` — REQ-006

MINOR: `docs/reference/infrastructure-manifests.md` reclassifies Chaos Mesh as a kind-only addon, points at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml`, and removes the chaos-mesh rows from the always-on cross-reference and Namespaces tables — REQ-006
MINOR: `architecture/docs/09-implementation/10-chaos-e2e-testing.md` reflects the WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true opt-in flag (replacing the old `SKIP_CHAOS_MESH=true` reference) — REQ-006
MINOR: `.planwerk/review_patterns/document-intentional-environment-divergence-in-overlays.md` notes the CC-0097 follow-up that chaos-mesh is now kind-only — REQ-006
MINOR: PR description records before/after wall-clock timing for `make deploy-infra` (target: ≥30s saved on warm-cache laptop, ≥60s on cold CI runner) — acceptance criterion 3
MINOR: All bash test stubs follow the existing `tests/unit/hack/` conventions (BATS, mock-based); no actual cluster required for unit tests — TEST_COVERAGE
MINOR: Every new file carries the SPDX Apache-2.0 3-line header and a `Feature: CC-0097` reference per FEATURE_ID_REQUIRED
implementation_notes:
Architectural decision: model the Chaos Mesh opt-in on the well-established prior art for kind-only addons in this repo: Headlamp (CC-0017), Flux Web UI (CC-0086), OpenBao UI (CC-0082), and Envoy Gateway + openstack-gw (CC-0088). All four live exclusively under deploy/kind/base/ (or its sibling overlays) and are absent from the production deploy/flux-system/ resource list. Chaos Mesh is the last platform-level addon still installed unconditionally, and CC-0097 closes that asymmetry.

Flag semantics: WITH_CHAOS_MESH mirrors WITH_FLUX_CLI exactly — a single string-equality test against `true` (matching hack/install-test-deps.sh:292). Defensive parity: any non-`true` value is treated as false. This keeps the Quick Start fast and the on-ramp single-knob simple.

Kustomize layout: a new opt-in overlay at `deploy/kind/chaos-mesh/kustomization.yaml` references the existing `deploy/flux-system/sources/chaos-mesh.yaml` and `deploy/flux-system/releases/chaos-mesh.yaml` via relative paths. The `chaos-mesh` Namespace (with the privileged PodSecurity label and rationale comment from current `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml:44-53`) moves into the new overlay so the privileged label only lands when chaos-mesh is actually deployed. The kind-tuning HelmRelease patch (containerd runtime, dashboard disabled, reduced resource requests) at current `deploy/kind/base/kustomization.yaml:108-142` MUST move into the new overlay because once chaos-mesh leaves the base resource list the patch has no target and kustomize refuses to render.

Chainsaw posture: the chaos-mesh assertion currently at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/infra-stack-health/chainsaw-test.yaml:169-190` (Step 5) moves to a sibling suite at `tests/e2e/infrastructure/chaos-mesh-health/chainsaw-test.yaml` modeled on the existing CC-0086 `flux-web-health` pattern: `metadata.labels.overlay: kind` for selector-based exclusion + a runtime `kubectl get ns chaos-mesh` presence guard for default-safety. The two gates are belt-and-braces — either alone addresses the requirement, both together make the suite default-safe regardless of invocation.

CI threading: `.github/actions/setup-e2e-infra/action.yaml` is a composite action consumed by both `e2e-infra` and `e2e-chaos` jobs. Threading the flag means adding it to the env block of the `Deploy infrastructure stack` step. Calling jobs that omit WITH_CHAOS_MESH get the script default false; the e2e-chaos job must explicitly set WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true on the step that calls the composite action.

Diagnostics safety: `hack/ci-dump-diagnostics.sh` line 38-39 already uses `2>/dev/null || true` for the chaos-daemon describe — but under `set -euo pipefail` a missing namespace renders the kubectl describe a no-op string-printer rather than an error. The fix is a presence guard that emits a clear SKIP line so log readers know it was intentional.

No backwards-compatibility shim is needed: this is a developer-environment opt-in, not an API change, and there are no external consumers of the flux-system overlay's chaos-mesh entries (the `tests/e2e-chaos/` suites consume the deployed cluster, not the manifest set).

Data flow:

  Default path (WITH_CHAOS_MESH=false):
    user → make deploy-infra → hack/deploy-infra.sh
      → preflight_checks
      → (skip load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules)
      → kind create cluster
      → kubectl apply flux-operator install.yaml + namespaces.yaml + fluxinstance.yaml
      → kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base   ← no chaos-mesh resources rendered
      → wait_for_helmreleases ... (no chaos-mesh in argument list)
      → cluster ready, no chaos-mesh namespace

  Opt-in path (WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true):
    user → WITH_CHAOS_MESH=true make deploy-infra → hack/deploy-infra.sh
      → preflight_checks
      → load_chaos_mesh_kernel_modules
      → kind create cluster
      → kubectl apply flux-operator install.yaml + namespaces.yaml + fluxinstance.yaml
      → kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/base
      → kubectl apply -k deploy/kind/chaos-mesh   ← Namespace + HelmRepository + HelmRelease with kind-tuning patch
      → wait_for_helmreleases ... chaos-mesh ...
      → cluster ready with chaos-mesh

Avoidance of pitfalls:
  - Do NOT delete `deploy/flux-system/{sources,releases}/chaos-mesh.yaml` — the new overlay references them.
  - Do NOT leave the kind-tuning patch in the base overlay — kustomize will refuse to render once the target HelmRelease is gone.
  - Do NOT auto-deploy chaos-mesh from `make e2e-chaos` — explicit opt-in is the design.
  - Do NOT change the `tests/e2e-chaos/` suite contents — they are byte-for-byte identical post-change.
